name: Sui Mai Pork Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Rice Wine or Dry Sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. The 1/2 lb pork loin should be ground with 1 ounce of fatback.
2. Cover mushrooms with boiling water and soak for 15 minutes. Drain, trim and discard tough stem ends and finely mince caps. Blanch bamboo shoots for 1 minute in boiling water; drain, pat dry and mince finely.
3. In a large bowl, combine pork, mushrooms, bamboo shoots, sesame oil, rice wine, cornstarch, egg white, salt, sugar and pepper until well mixed. Form into 30 1-inch balls.
4. Trim the corner of won ton wrappers to form circles; cover with plastic wrap to prevent them from drying out.
5. To assemble, place a meatball in the center of each wrapper and bring up sides, to form an open topped basket. Flatten the top of each meatball with a butter knife dipped in water and flatten bottoms of dumpling so they stand upright. Place on a lightly oiled plate and set in bamboo steamer or on an inverted heatproof bowl or trivet set in a wok filled with 2 inches of boiling water.
6. Cover and steam for 5 to 8 minutes or until done. Serve meatballs hot and garnish plate with sprigs of fresh corianders. Makes 30 dumplings.
